science.title: The Probiotic Breakthrough for Natural Anxiety Relief and Better Mental Health
science.summary: A surprising gut-brain discovery suggests that anxiety could one day be treated with specially designed probiotics. Could anxiety be shaped, at least in part, by tiny organisms living in the gut? Research from Duke-NUS Medical School and the National Neuroscience Institute of Singapore points to a striking connection b

science.title: Hidden Solar Storms May Be Lighting Japan’s Skies With Massive Red Auroras
science.summary: Unusually tall red auroras over Japan may be revealing that some solar storms are stronger than scientists realize. A study published in the Journal of Space Weather and Space Climate reports that red auroras observed over Japan extended to altitudes of roughly 500-800 kilometers above Earth. The discovery was made by

science.title: Everyone thought these helmets were Roman until scientists uncovered the truth
science.summary: Researchers have solved a decades-old mystery by showing that a cache of 43 helmets found off the Spanish coast is medieval, not Roman. The remarkable discovery exposes a thriving weapons trade network that connected Mediterranean powers during a time of piracy, warfare, and growing demand for military equipment.

science.title: Scientists finally complete Schrödinger’s 100-year-old color theory
science.summary: Researchers have finally resolved a key problem in a 100-year-old theory of color, showing that the qualities we perceive in colors are intrinsic to the mathematics of color space itself. The discovery sharpens our understanding of human vision and could lead to more precise color technologies and visualizations.

science.title: Scientists discovered something surprising about french fries and diabetes
science.summary: French fries may be the real potato problem. A large study tracking more than 205,000 people for nearly 40 years found that eating three servings of fries per week was linked to a 20% higher risk of developing type 2 diabetes, while baked, boiled, or mashed potatoes showed no significant increase in risk. The research

tech.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
tech.summary: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more com

tech.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
tech.summary: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

tech.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone could be just around the corner
tech.summary: The iOS 27 developer beta includes code that references the fold state and screen angle of a device.

tech.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
tech.summary: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

tech.title: The UK Is Betting on a Billion-Dollar AI Supercomputer to Kick Its Addiction to US Tech
tech.summary: The British government thinks a state-backed infrastructure initiative will help supercharge homegrown chip startups.

medicine.title: Scientists finally crack an “undruggable” pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
medicine.summary: For decades, pancreatic cancer has been one of the most lethal cancers, with few effective treatment options. A new drug, daraxonrasib, targets the KRAS mutation that fuels most pancreatic tumors—something many scientists once thought couldn't be done. In a major clinical trial, the treatment nearly doubled survival fo

medicine.title: Doctors thought this kidney drug helped some patients. It may help millions more.
medicine.summary: A trio of major studies found that finerenone may protect the kidneys and heart in far more people than previously thought. The drug significantly slowed kidney disease progression and reduced the risks of kidney failure, heart failure, cardiovascular death, and overall mortality. Researchers saw benefits not only in p

medicine.title: Scientists discover why ozempic may not work for some people
medicine.summary: Scientists have identified genetic variants that may make some people less responsive to GLP-1 drugs used to treat Type 2 diabetes. Roughly 10% of the population carries these variants, which appear to cause a mysterious form of "GLP-1 resistance." In several clinical trials, carriers were significantly less likely to

medicine.title: Fear-based messaging in anti-smoking campaigns can drive behavioral change, study finds
medicine.summary: Fear can be used strategically in public health messaging to encourage people to quit smoking and avoid tobacco use, according to a joint study by researchers from the University of Sharjah and the University of Jordan. The study examines how the U.S.-based public health agency Centers for Disease Control and Preventio

medicine.title: Scientists found a new Alzheimer’s trigger and a drug that stops it
medicine.summary: Researchers have identified a new Alzheimer’s target and created an experimental compound that blocks a damaging process inside brain cells. In mice, the treatment slowed nerve cell loss, reduced Alzheimer’s-related changes, and even appeared to promote healthier aging.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics
mathematics.summary: Grothendieck is revered in the world of math; outside of it, he’s known for his unusual life, if he’s known at all. But what were his actual mathematical contributions? The post How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test
space.summary: A new propulsion system feeds the same "monopropellant" fuel into electrical and chemical thrusters, potentially saving on mass and complications for future missions to Mars.

space.title: How NASA Science and Artemis Are Shaping the 2026 FIFA World Cup
space.summary: As the FIFA World Cup approaches, NASA is bringing space science and engineering to soccer fans worldwide. From June 11 to July 19, 2026, NASA will host an exhibit at FIFA Fan Festival™ Houston where visitors can learn how research aboard the In

space.title: Artemis III Crew Announced
space.summary: NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Luca Parmitano, and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio take a photo together on June 9, 2026. The four were announced as the Artemis III crew. NASA’s Artemis III mission in low Earth orbit will test integrated operations between the Or

space.title: NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members
space.summary: Taking another step toward one of the most complex human spaceflight missions in recent history, NASA on Tuesday provided new Artemis III details and announced the four prime crew members and a backup for the test flight. The mission will undertake a series of challenging tests in Earth orbit in 2027, essential fo

space.title: NASA reveals Artemis 3 astronaut crew
space.summary: NASA named the astronauts who will launch on its Artemis 3 mission next year to test docking procedures in Earth orbit ahead of a planned moon landing in 2028.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Make "Live" Observation of a Nearby Protoplanetary Disk's Rotation
astronomy.summary: Ever since the first protoplanetary disk was discovered in 1984 around the star Beta Pictoris, these objects have presented astronomers with laboratories to study the births and evolution of worlds around distant stars. astronomy.title: June Podcast: Dance of the Planets
astronomy.summary: This month’s episode highlights the close pairing of Venus and Jupiter under way in the western sky after sunset. You'll also learn why astronomers are fixated on a star in Corona Borealis — and how to find a huge but dim constellation that will likely be new to you. So grab curiosity and come along on this month’s Sky

astronomy.title: Orbiting Stars Give Clues to a Quiescent Black Hole's Mass
astronomy.summary: How do you measure the mass of a dormant black hole in the early Universe? That's a question astronomers at University College London (UCL) and Carnegie scientists wanted to answer about a distant object that is invisible. So, they turned to James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) studies of the region around the black hole

astronomy.title: The Cosmic Web Like You've Never Seen it Before
astronomy.summary: Using data from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers led by researchers at the University of California, Riverside have produced the most detailed map of the cosmic web ever made, tracing the network of galaxies all the way back to when the universe was one billion years old.

astronomy.title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test
astronomy.summary: A new propulsion system feeds the same "monopropellant" fuel into electrical and chemical thrusters, potentially saving on mass and complications for future missions to Mars.

architecture.title: KOPA / Ivy Studio
architecture.summary: Located in the heart of Pointe-Saint-Charles, a historic industrial neighborhood in Montreal, Kopa establishes itself as a premier destination for community and indoor racket sports. Occupying a freestanding, single-story industrial building of roughly 20,000 square feet, the project required a significant structural i

architecture.title: The Lasting Impact of Architectural Education: Training Professionals to Question Convention
architecture.summary: Architectural schools usually leave lasting marks on their students, shaping their style and critical inquiry long after formal education has ended. architecture.title: "The Century of Gehry": Frank Gehry Retrospective Opens at the Serralves Museum in Porto
architecture.summary: From June 12 to December 20, 2026, the Serralves Museum of Contemporary Art in Porto, Portugal, will be hosting a retrospective exhibition dedicated to the career of Frank Gehry (1929-2025). Titled The Century of Gehry, the exhibition presents to the public original large-scale models, sculptures, drawings, furniture,

architecture.title: Ara Manor / Reincarnation
architecture.summary: Emerging from the green landscape of Narsingdi, Ara Manor is conceived as a retreat where architecture dissolves into climate, vegetation, water, and light. Designed as a three-storied vacation residence for the owner of Crony Group, the project explores how contemporary domestic architecture in Bangladesh can remain d

architecture.title: Wadhal subverts suburban architecture for home in west London
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Wadhal has completed Wade House in London, using textured brickwork, pared-back fenestration and concealed guttering to put a subtly contemporary spin on typical suburban architecture. construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Yorkshire Water appoints Jacobs to £32M AI framework
construction.summary: Engineering and construction services firm Jacobs has been appointed to Yorkshire Water’s artificial intelligence (AI) services framework. The post Yorkshire Water appoints Jacobs to £32M AI framework appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: Healthcare joined data centers in boost to May construction planning
construction.summary: The increase builds on last month’s growth after a slow start to the year, according to Dodge Construction Network.

construction.title: Data center construction boom driving historic manufacturing opportunities
construction.summary: From multinationals like ABB and Siemens to small, family-owned businesses, manufacturers are staking their claim in this lucrative new vertical through investments and acquisitions. The question now is how long it will last.
